FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Namespace FluentGpu.Foundation

Classes

ArenaAllocator

Per-frame bump allocator. O(1) Reset(). Spans are invalidated by Reset (and by a grow).

BlurHash

BlurHash decoder — turns a compact ~20–30-char string into a tiny blurred preview (the LQIP placeholder used by Next.js blurDataURL, SwiftUI/Nuke BlurHash). The engine uploads the decoded tile as an image's initial texture so a recognizable blur shows instantly while the full-res art decodes off-thread. Pure math (no deps), AOT-safe. Algorithm: github.com/woltapp/blurhash.

CharEventArgs

Character (text) input passed to OnCharInput handlers — the layout/IME-resolved Unicode codepoint, distinct from the raw virtual-key of KeyEventArgs (Win32 splits WM_KEYDOWN from WM_CHAR; we mirror that).

Diag

The standardized, reusable engine-wide diagnostics facility. Every subsystem instruments through it the same way: Diag.Count("text.glyph","rasterized"), Diag.Set("d3d12","glyphInstances", n), Diag.Event("rhi","device-lost"), using (Diag.Time("frame","record")) { … }.

COST MODEL: the recording methods are [Conditional("DEBUG"), Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")], so on a Release build (with neither symbol defined) the compiler removes the call site AND the argument evaluation entirely — a Diag.Set("text.atlas","nonZero", ExpensiveScan()) costs literally nothing in production. Define FLUENTGPU_DIAG to keep diagnostics in a Release build; toggle at runtime with the AppContext switch "FluentGpu.Diagnostics" or by setting Enabled. Route output by setting Sink.

DragEventArgs

Drag-reorder lifecycle payload for OnDragStarted/OnDragDelta/OnDragCompleted: the pointer in the dragged node's CURRENT box (Local stays ≈ the grab offset) and in window space, the accumulated gesture translation since the arming press, and the smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) for flick/settle decisions. ONE instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move) — handlers must copy fields they keep, never hold the reference.

DragSession

THE live drag session (E5-L2): ONE mutable instance owned by Input.DragDropContext, opened when an L1 drag promotes on a chain carrying a DragSource (payload resolved once), updated per pointer move, handed to every DropTargetSpec handler, and cleared (incl. the Payload GC edge) when the gesture ends. Handlers copy what they keep — never hold the reference across gestures.

DragSource

E5-L2 typed drag SOURCE spec (BoxEl.Draggable — the Flutter Draggable / react-beautiful-dnd model; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop): Kind is a string discriminator so target accept-tests are cast-free; PayloadFactory resolves the typed payload ONCE when the L1 press promotes past the drag box (never per move). Trimming-safe: plain delegates.

DropTargetSpec

E5-L2 drop TARGET spec (BoxEl.DropTarget — Flutter DragTarget / SwiftUI dropDestination): receives sessions whose Kind is in AcceptKinds. Discovery is hit-test-chain based — per pointer move the engine picks the NEAREST enabled accepting target under the pointer (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor). OnEnter/OnLeave fire on hover transitions, OnOver every move while inside, OnDrop on release over it (BEFORE the L1 completion). The DragSession argument is THE one live reused instance — copy what you keep.

Easings

Evaluates an Easing curve at normalized time t (0..1).

KeyEventArgs

Keyboard event passed to node handlers during tunnel/bubble routing. Handled stops propagation. Carries the modifier chord and the auto-repeat flag (Win32 lParam bit 30) so editors can do Ctrl+arrow word navigation and Shift+arrow selection without re-querying the keyboard.

Keys

Well-known virtual-key codes (Win32 VK_*) used by the input router.

ObjectPool<T>

Edge-only recycle pool for managed objects (Component / RenderContext). Cap-bounded.

PngWriter

Minimal pure-managed PNG encoder for debug frame captures — no WIC / System.Drawing, AOT-safe, zero new deps. Emits an opaque RGB PNG (color type 2) from a tightly-packed top-down BGRA8 buffer (the D3D12 readback format). Used only by the --screenshot tooling path; never on the hot frame path.

PointerEventArgs

Position-aware pointer-press payload for OnPointerPressed: local coords + the click count (1/2/3 — the dispatcher tracks double/triple-click timing and slop), the modifier chord, the button (0=left 1=right 2=middle) and the device kind. Allocated only on an actual press (cold user-gesture edge).

SlabAllocator<T>

Stable, generation-versioned slab over T. Index 0 is reserved as the null slot, so default(Handle) is null. Generation is bumped on Free (ABA defense). O(1) alloc/free via an intrusive int free-list. Pointer-bearing T uses a side _next array (we always do, here).

SpanRun

A registered span run: the immutable per-span shaping overlays plus the seam-published layout artifacts. Lives in Shared; referenced from TextStyle.SpanRunId (the measure-cache / shaped-run-cache key), so a style change ⇒ a fresh run id ⇒ every downstream cache self-invalidates.

SpanRunRects

An immutable snapshot of a span run's laid-out rect artifacts at one wrap width. Published whole by the text seam on a measure-cache miss (content/width change) and read lock-free by the recorder (decoration bars) and the input dispatcher (hyperlink cursor/click) — steady frames never re-touch the font seam.

SpanRunTable

Process-wide registry of span runs — the interner-style side channel that lets the multi-run style overlay ride an int through the POD pipeline (TextStyle.SpanRunId → DrawList DrawGlyphRunCmd.SpanRunId) the way strings ride StringId through StringTable.

Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9, the StringTable discipline): chunked backing store — chunks are allocated once and never moved; a release-store of the count publishes each new slot, the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing. The UI thread is the single writer (reconciler, at element-change time — never in frame phases 6–13); the render-side glyph path resolves the previous frame's ids concurrently.

Static Shared (deliberate): unlike StringTable (host-instanced and threaded through every constructor), the render-side consumer reaches a span run only through the POD id baked in the draw stream — there is no host seam to hand an instance through. One process = one UI thread = one writer, ids burn monotonically and are never reused, so a process-global table is safe by the same argument that makes the ids safe.

Reclamation: owners (the scene's span-text side-table) pair AddRef(int)/Release(int); a run whose count hits 0 has its slot cleared after a creation-distance quarantine (≥ FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.QuarantineDistance newer runs registered — far beyond the in-flight frame depth at any realistic churn), so a draw list recorded just before the release still resolves. A cleared id resolves null → consumers fall back to the base style. Ids are never reused.

StringTable

String interner with REF-COUNTED reclamation. Lives in Foundation so Render can resolve without depending on Scene.

 <p><b>Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9):</b> the UI thread interns (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Intern(System.String)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>, the single writer) while
 the render thread resolves (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Resolve(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>) the previous frame's ids concurrently. The backing store is therefore
 <i>chunked</i>: chunks are allocated once and never resized or moved, so a concurrent reader can never observe a torn or
 reallocated array (a plain <code>List<string></code> would race on its geometric Add-realloc). A release-store of the count
 publishes each new slot; the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing, so it only ever reads fully-written slots.</p>

 <p><b>Reclamation (virtualization workloads):</b> a 100k-row virtual list streams unique row text through the table —
 append-only interning is an unbounded leak. Owners (the scene's text columns) pair <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.AddRef(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>/<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Release(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>;
 when an id's count hits 0 its map entry is removed (a re-intern of the same content gets a NEW id) and the slot is cleared
 after a reader quarantine (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Tick" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> per frame, far beyond the in-flight frame depth). <b>Ids are never reused</b> —
 reclaimed ids stay burned, so a stale id can never alias different content (the shaped-run cache keys on ids and ages out in
 well under the id space). Fully-dead chunks free their backing array. Strings never AddRef'd are simply permanent.</p>
WheelEventArgs

Element-level wheel payload for OnPointerWheel (WinUI PointerWheelChanged): the platform wheel delta — the same value the viewport scroll path consumes — plus the modifier chord. Setting Handled stops the dispatcher from scrolling the enclosing viewport (NumberBox value-stepping inside a scrollable form, NumberBox.cpp:578-597 OnNumberBoxScroll marks the routed event handled). Unhandled, the wheel keeps walking up to the next handler / the nearest scrollable (routed-event semantics). Allocated per wheel event (cold edge).

Structs

AcrylicSpec

A per-node acrylic (frosted glass): the engine samples the canvas behind the node, resolves transparent backdrop through Fallback, blurs it (BlurSigma), then applies WinUI's luminosity/tint recipe. Realized by the PushLayer/PopLayer backdrop subsystem.

Affine2D

2x3 affine (local→parent). 2.5D/perspective out of scope (per spec).

ArcSpec

A circular-arc stroke (WinUI ProgressRing — a trimmed, round-capped ring). The arc is centred in the node's box with radius (min(W,H)-Thickness)/2, a Thickness-wide stroke, swept from StartDeg for SweepDeg degrees (0° = 12 o'clock, clockwise). The recorder emits a DrawArc; the GPU draws the SDF arc with round caps. A full ring is SweepDeg = 360.

BrushHandle

Typed wrapper for brushes interned in the BrushTable.

ColorF

Straight-alpha sRGB color (P8: brush color = straight-alpha sRGB float4).

CornerRadius4
CursorId
DiagScope

Timing scope from Time(string, string). Zero-work on Release (the Diag.CompiledIn const folds out).

EasingSpec
Edges4

Per-edge thickness (margin/padding/border). [InlineArray]-shaped logically; struct of 4 floats here.

EnterExit

Presented-space terminal for an inserted/removed node: where it animates FROM on enter / TO on exit (offset + scale + opacity), relative to its laid-out rect. Active=false ⇒ the node simply snaps in/out.

GradientSpec

A gradient fill. AngleDeg is the linear axis (0 = left-to-right, 90 = top-to-bottom). Up to 4 stops are carried into the POD draw command; extras are ignored. When set on a node it supersedes the solid fill at record time.

GradientStop

One gradient stop: a normalized position (0..1 along the gradient axis) and its color.

Handle

Generational handle — 8 bytes {u32 index, u32 gen}. The canonical FluentGpu handle layout.

ImageTransition

The placeholder→image reveal transition (the cross-fade) — spec'd with the engine's shared motion vocabulary (a duration in ms + an Easing curve) so image reveals animate exactly like every other control. Override per-image via ImageEl.Transition, or change the app-wide Default once at startup. Set DurationMs = 0 (or use None) to DISABLE the fade — the image then appears instantly.

KeyAccelerator

A keyboard accelerator chord (WinUI KeyboardAccelerator): Key + Mods invoke the owning node's click handler from anywhere (dispatched after focused routing leaves the key unhandled).

LayoutTransition

The whole authoring surface (interned POD). Channels × dynamics × size-mode × enter/exit compose orthogonally — translate / scale / rotate / opacity / clip-reveal all fall out, with no per-control special cases.

NativeHandle

POD across the PAL/RHI seam — never a boxed object. The D3D12 leaf switches on Kind.

NodeHandle

Zero-cost typed wrapper over Handle for nodes in the SceneStore.

Point2
PolylineStrokeSpec

A retained stroked polyline with normalized trim controls. Points are local DIP coordinates inside the node's box; trim values are path-distance fractions (0..1), so animation can reveal the stroke in draw order.

RectF

Axis-aligned rectangle in DIPs. Local to its node (P8: Bounds are node-LOCAL).

ShadowSpec

A drop shadow / elevation descriptor (CSS box-shadow shape). Lives in Foundation so Scene/Render/Reconciler can read it without referencing Dsl. Carried by an elevated node; the recorder emits a DrawShadow beneath the fill.

Size2
SpanRect

One laid-out rect artifact of a span run (paragraph-node-local DIP): the line-fragment geometry of a decorated/link span. Kind is the single SpanStyle flag bit it serves — Link rects are the FULL line-fragment band (hit-testing, Hand cursor); Underline/Strikethrough rects are the positioned BARS (the recorder fills them verbatim, no font-seam touch at record time).

SpanStyle

The POD shaping overlay for one span of a span run: the UTF-16 char range [Start, End) of the CONCATENATED paragraph text plus the style deltas the text seam applies over the base TextStyle. Weight 0 / SizeDip 0 / FontFamily empty / Color A==0 = inherit the base. Built by the reconciler from TextSpans; resolved by both text backends and the glyph renderer through Shared, so measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render for span runs too.

StringId

4-byte interned string id. Id 0 = empty. No string reaches the paint path.

TextSpan

One inline run of a rich-text paragraph (the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines Run/Bold/Hyperlink model, rtb-01). A SpanTextEl paragraph is an array of these; the WHOLE paragraph shapes as ONE flow (spans concatenated, wrapped together — exactly how WinUI lays a paragraph's inline collection, RichTextBlock.g.h:63 get_Blocks).

Weight 0 / Size null / FontFamily null / Color A==0 all mean "inherit the paragraph base". OnClick non-null makes this a HYPERLINK span: the engine resolves the Hand cursor over the span's laid-out rects and fires the action on click/tap — WinUI's inline Hyperlink (SetCursor(MouseCursorHand) on hyperlink hover, RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488). Accent foreground + underline styling is the CALLER's job (HyperlinkForeground theming, generic.xaml:1120-1122).

No italic axis: the shaper resolves faces by (family, weight) only — DWRITE_FONT_STYLE_NORMAL is fixed (TextLayoutEngine face resolution); FontStyle arrives with the italics workstream (plan §Text display).

TrackSize
TransitionDynamics

The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an EasingSpec, so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to FluentDecelerate). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults.

Enums

AutomationRole

The semantic role of a node, set by the control factories (a button IS a BoxEl, so the role is how it announces itself). Carried on the input/a11y column; surfaced to the future UIA layer (ControlType), devtools, and UI tests. Default None = a plain box/container.

DropEffect

The semantic outcome of an IN-APP drop (E5-L2 — the Flutter/SwiftUI model, never OLE): advisory — the engine sets Move while over an accepting target, None otherwise; targets may refine it in OnEnter/OnOver.

DynamicTextKind

Retained text slots whose contents are refreshed by the host without re-rendering or relayout.

DynamicsKind

Spring (velocity-carrying, interruptible — the default) or an eased fixed-duration tween.

Easing

Easing curves — a FOUNDATIONAL motion primitive shared by every layer that animates: the animation-track engine, declarative transitions, and the image placeholder→art cross-fade. Living in Foundation lets any subsystem (Dsl, Scene, Render, Animation) spec a transition with the same vocabulary. Includes the real Fluent 2 / WinUI curves.

FlexAlign

Cross-axis alignment (flexbox align-items / align-self). Auto on a child = inherit the container's align-items.

FlexJustify

Main-axis distribution of free space (flexbox justify-content).

GradientShape
ImageFit

How an image's pixels map into its (possibly differently-shaped) layout box — the CSS object-fit model, cleaner than WinUI's Stretch because box-sizing is a separate concern (see AspectRatio). Default Cover (what album art / thumbnails want): never distorts, never letterboxes.

KeyModifiers

Keyboard modifier state, captured per input event at the platform pump (Win32 GetKeyState).

LineStacking

How an explicit LineHeight interacts with the font-natural line box (mirrors WinUI LineStackingStrategy; BaselineToBaseline is not modeled). MaxHeight — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:16) — makes the line advance max(font-natural, LineHeight); BlockLineHeight makes the line advance exactly LineHeight (lines may visually overlap when smaller than natural).

NativeHandleKind
NodeFlags

Per-node dirty + state bits (a single 32-bit SceneStore column). Three orthogonal dirty axes.

PointerKind

The physical pointer device class an event came from (WinUI PointerDeviceType) — controls touch-vs-mouse behavioral splits (RatingControl hover scale, touch paddings).

SizeAnchor

Which edge of a Reflow node its CONTENT is anchored to while the size animates. Leading = content stays put, the far edge sweeps (a wipe). Trailing = the content's end edge rides the animated edge (the WinUI Expander "slide out from under the header": at reveal 0 the content sits fully behind the leading edge; its trailing rounded corners stay visible mid-motion). Applied by the recorder as a child-group offset — compositor-composed, no per-child knowledge.

SizeMode

How a SIZE change is animated. Auto picks per node: pure-chrome leaf → ScaleCorrect; a text/wrapping subtree under budget → Relayout; everything else → Reveal.

TextLineBounds

Which vertical band a text line reports as its box (mirrors WinUI TextLineBounds, minimum set — TrimToCapHeight/TrimToBaseline individually are not modeled). Full — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:17) — is the font-natural ascent..descent box; Tight trims the box to cap-height..baseline so vertical centering is optical (e.g. PersonPicture initials, PersonPicture.xaml:66).

TextTrim

How overflowing text is truncated (mirrors WinUI TextTrimming). Applies to the last visible line.

TextWrap

How a text run breaks across lines (mirrors WinUI TextWrapping).

TrackKind

A CSS-Grid track sizing function. (Lives in Foundation so both the DSL and the layout/scene see it.)

TransitionChannels

The general layout-transition spec — the entire authoring surface for "animate this node's layout changes". A plain POD (no closures) so it is hot-path safe, and it lives in Foundation (below the AnimEngine, which reads it and converts the dynamics to its internal spring/tween). It attaches to a node via BoxEl.Animate (or RenderContext.UseLayoutAnimation); the host diffs the node's presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the presented geometry toward target through these channels/dynamics — no relayout, no per-frame re-render, and zero control-type knowledge.

FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
